candidates.title: Chuckwagon racing legend Tom Glass dies at 77
candidates.description: Tom Glass, a monumental figure in Western Canadian sports history, has died after suffering a stroke at 77. As "the face of chuckwagon racing," Glass dominated the sport locally and internationally during the 1980s and 1990s.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Evacuation order issued for Lynn Lake, Man., as wildfire expected to reach town Sunday
candidates.description: As of Friday, an out-of-control wildfire south of the town has quickly grown to 1,600 hectares in size since it was detected on Thursday, according to a provincial fire status report.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canadian Army holds sunset ceremony to deactivate 5th Division
candidates.description: The 5th Division of Canadian Army is transitioning with new structure and missions. A ceremony was held on Saturday in Halifax to commemorate its accomplishments in the Atlantic region.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Nearly 20 years after her murder in Quebec, an arrest in Guinea gives her loved ones hope
candidates.description: Rachelle Wrathmall, 31, was found dead in her Lennoxville home in Sherbrooke, Que., in June of 2007. She had been stabbed to death. The day before she was found, her husband left Canada and eventually returned to his home country.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 66 fires currently burning in the Northwest Territories, evacuation alert remains in place for Fort Simpson
candidates.description: "Thousands of lightning strikes" mean a very busy weekend, says N.W.T. fire. Fort Simpson fire remains main concern, as well as fire near the Alberta border that threatens the highway. High heat and the risk of dry lightning continues for the area around Wood Buffalo National Park, Fort Smith and in the Sahtú and Dehcho.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Refining cobalt in Cobalt, Ont.? That's the plan for this northern Ontario town
candidates.description: A facility near Cobalt, Ont., has been tapped to be the home of North America's first battery-grade cobalt refinery. Projected to be fully operational by late 2027, the plant will import and process mined cobalt — experts say it could bring Canada into an industry dominated by China.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Yukon fans celebrate hometown star Gavin McKenna's draft to the Maple Leafs
candidates.description: For Yukon hockey fans, it was like a dream come true on Friday when hometown hero Gavin McKenna was the first overall pick in the NHL draft.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Summer of unsettled weather expected across the Prairies
candidates.description: It's been a cooler start to summer in the southern Prairies, all due to multiple low pressure systems hovering over the region this month. It's also the cause of unsettled weather, which continues into July.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Former envoy to U.S. doesn't think 'serious Americans' will get involved in Alta. separatism
candidates.description: As Alberta pushes ahead with a fall referendum to decide whether an actual referendum on separating from Canada is worth the effort, Canada's former U.S. ambassador says she doesn't think "serious Americans" will get involved in the debate.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta says pipeline proposal on track for July 1 deadline, as uncertainty hangs over project
candidates.description: As a Canada Day deadline fast approaches for a pipeline submission to Ottawa's major projects office, questions still surround whether the proposal will be ready in time, and if the project is commercially viable.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Construction set to start on first road to Ontario's Ring of Fire mineral deposit
candidates.description: An environmental assessment has been approved for a road the Ontario government says is vital in accessing critical minerals in the Ring of Fire, which Ontario Premier Doug Ford says will add $22 billion to the economy and create 70,000 jobs. Here's what we know about the project and concerns expressed by neighbouring Neskantaga First Nation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. launches probe into StubHub over World Cup resale ticket fiasco
candidates.description: British Columbia's attorney general announced an inquiry into online ticketing site StubHub, days after a CBC News investigation that revealed a mass failure by the ticket reseller platform and cancellation of thousands of FIFA World Cup tickets in Vancouver, Toronto and host cities across North America.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ex-husband found guilty of murdering Tatjana Stefanski in B.C. Interior
candidates.description: Vitali Stefanski has been found guilty of second-degree murder in the 2024 death of his ex-wife, Tatjana Stefanski.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. woman sets record at 4,400-km Tour Divide bike race
candidates.description: A cyclist from Kelowna has broken the women's record at the Tour Divide, an event many consider the pinnacle of offroad ultra-distance bike racing.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Registered sex offender pleads guilty to sexual assault of 3-year-old in Welland, Ont., other charges
candidates.description: Daniel Senecal was arrested on Aug. 31, 2025, after police were called to a home in Welland, Ont., for a "medical assistance call involving a child." The 26-year-old pleaded guilty this week in St. Catharines court to multiple charges.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Home goods store Flying Tiger lands in Canada, where competition awaits
candidates.description: The Danish brand that sells all-things cheap and cute, like dish towels in fruit motifs, animal-shaped erasers and jewelry boxes resembling fancy sofas for low prices, opened its first Canadian store at Toronto's Eaton Centre on Friday. Experts say the brand will face competition from the likes of Miniso and Daiso — Asian entrants which also came to the Canadian market in recent years.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada hopes to avoid slow start in World Cup knockout match against South Africa
candidates.description: Forward Tani Oluwaseyi knows Canada can't afford another slow start when it faces South Africa on Sunday in a FIFA World Cup knockout match in Los Angeles.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Russians are turning on Putin as drones hit Moscow. Does it matter?
candidates.description: After the largest Ukrainian drone offensive on the Russian capital and other strikes on energy infrastructure, confidence in Putin's leadership has seemed to waver among civilians and prominent nationalists alike. But analysts are skeptical whether it is enough to turn the tide.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran says it has launched retaliatory strikes on U.S. bases in Kuwait, Bahrain
candidates.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said early on ‌Sunday that its navy and ​aerospace forces had launched joint missile and drone ​operations targeting U.S. military sites in ‌Kuwait and Bahrain in response to recent U.S. strikes against Iran.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heat wave in Europe shatters records in Denmark, Switzerland and Czech Republic
candidates.description: Temperatures hit record highs from Switzerland to the Czech Republic and Denmark on Saturday, as a heat wave that baked western European countries this week moves to central and eastern parts of the continent.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: At the 2026 World Cup, Iraqi Shias reconcile celebration with grief during holy month
candidates.description: Soccer fans usually feel upbeat emotions when watching their national team play in a FIFA World Cup for the first time in 40 years. But this year, some Iraqi supporters have had to balance cheering the national team with observing the Shia Islamic month of mourning.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump administration allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to 'trusted' U.S. organizations
candidates.description: Anthropic said on Friday that the U.S. government has allowed it to release its powerful Claude Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model to some "trusted" U.S. organizations, partially reversing an order two weeks ago to suspend access over national security risks.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. strikes Iran in retaliation for attack on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
candidates.description: The U.S. struck Iran on Friday to respond to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries to begin working to end their months-long war and reopen the pivotal waterway.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: California appeals court upholds Weinstein's rape conviction, orders resentencing
candidates.description: An appeals court on Friday upheld Harvey Weinstein's 2022 rape and sexual assault conviction in California, but ordered his trial judge to resentence him.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: OpenAI restricts release of newest ChatGPT model to Trump-approved group during testing period
candidates.description: OpenAI says its new AI product, called GPT-5.6 Sol, will only be initially available to a "small group of trusted partners" approved by the Trump administration. The move comes after OpenAI's rival, Anthropic, took two of its models offline shortly after release to comply with a Trump directive.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Israel, Lebanon sign framework agreement with U.S. in 'first step' toward peace
candidates.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio joined Israel and Lebanon's ambassadors to the U.S. Friday to announce a framework agreement that was described as a first step toward peace following months of conflict between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: John Bolton, former Trump adviser, pleads guilty to 1 count of illegally retaining classified info
candidates.description: John Bolton, national security adviser during Donald Trump's first presidential administration, pleaded guilty on Friday to illegally retaining classified information, sealing a deal with federal prosecutors.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russia reports large-scale Ukrainian drone attack across 12 regions
candidates.description: Russian air defences intercepted 660 Ukrainian drones in a major nighttime attack on 12 Russian regions as well as the Russia-held Crimean peninsula, the Black Sea and the Azov Sea, Russia's Defence Ministry said Friday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why Europe can't air condition its way out of extreme heat
candidates.description: Hundreds of people are dead from Europe’s heatwave, in a continent where air conditioning is not the norm. But while ACs may save lives, they can also make the problem worse.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Driver in Christmas vehicle attack in Germany that killed 6 people sentenced to life in prison
candidates.description: A Saudi doctor was sentenced to life in prison in Germany on ‌Friday for killing six people and injuring hundreds, after he rammed a rented BMW into crowds at a historic market in the eastern city of Magdeburg days before Christmas in 2024.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements
candidates.description: Between 2001 and 2024, the FCAC says RBC failed to transfer credits from deactivated credit card accounts to customers' new ones. This resulted in incorrect monthly credit card statements.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'Every person saved is a miracle': Families call to trapped loved ones in region devastated by Venezuela quakes
candidates.description: Families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their loved ones are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
candidates.description: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
candidates.description: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
candidates.description: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
candidates.description: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
candidates.description: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Billionaire Leon Black walks out of Epstein investigation hearing
candidates.description: The House panel then issued subpoenas demanding the private equity tycoon, who co-founded Apollo Global Management, testify on camera and under oath.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Pete Buttigieg briefly separated from children after false police report
candidates.description: Buttigieg says the day he spent separated from his twins was "among the darkest hours of my life".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
candidates.description: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
candidates.description: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
candidates.description: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it
candidates.description: Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: GTA 6 will launch as download only - does that mean the disc is dead?
candidates.description: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?
candidates.description: Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
candidates.description: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: BBC reports from La Guaira, one of Venezuela's worst-hit areas
candidates.description: Vanessa Silva visited the coastal state, where rescuers are still working to find survivors under the rubble after twin earthquakes rocked the country.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tech firms are blaming AI for mega device and console price rises
candidates.description: Xbox consoles, Nintendo's new Switch 2 and Valve's Steam Deck are just some of the gadgets seeing price hikes in recent months.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
candidates.description: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
candidates.description: Forecasters have suggested that summer is likely to be warmer than average with an increased chance of more heatwaves, as Simon King explains.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Future generations being failed if more trees not planted, experts say
candidates.description: Statistics show the number of trees being planted is increasing, but conservationists say it is not meeting targets.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Fossil identified as first dinosaur ever found in Antarctica
candidates.description: Argentinian geologist Eduardo Olivero became the first scientist to find the remains of a dinosaur in Antarctica in 1986.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
candidates.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: New strikes test the Iran ceasefire
candidates.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak
candidates.description: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
candidates.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
candidates.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
candidates.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuelans in Colombia scramble to send aid as earthquakes death toll increases
candidates.description: Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed over 1,400 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: There's a beef about beef at the World Cup, as Argentina fans pour into Texas
candidates.description: It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. strikes multiple targets in Iran in retaliation for oil tanker attack
candidates.description: A recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran appears even more fragile following the latest hostilities in the region, which includes U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
candidates.description: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: This mindset shift can help you get better at using up your leftovers
candidates.description: Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: World Cup fans are missing games after their resale tickets fall through
candidates.description: The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
candidates.description: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
candidates.description: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
candidates.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised: "We have a whole-of-government response. It'll be big; it'll be fast; and it'll be effective."
candidates.author: NPR
